steps.title: Observe the equation form
steps.content: The equation $y = 16 + 14(x - 1)$ contains two additive terms: 16 and $14(x - 1)$.

steps.title: Apply commutativity to match a choice
steps.content: We can rearrange the terms using commutativity of addition: $16 + 14(x - 1) = 14(x - 1) + 16$. A Desmos table also checks the model: 1 book costs $16$, 2 books cost $30$, and 3 books cost $44$. Fitting $y_1\sim m(x_1-1)+b$ gives $m=14$ and $b=16$, which is $14(x-1)+16$. This matches choice C exactly — C is correct.
